up.method: calculated: this review reports maximum specific activity of S. fumaroxidans in co-culture with M. hungatei of 0.2 mol propionate/g[dw]/day (Scholten and Conrad 2000, cited secondary); converted 0.2 mol/gDW/day = 200 mmol/gDW/day / 24 h = 8.33 mmol/gDW/h

growth_records.cite: Westerholm et al. Syntrophic propionate-oxidizing bacteria in methanogenic systems. FEMS Microbiology Reviews (2022). DOI:10.1093/femsre/fuab057. [PMC8892533]
growth_records.snippet: maximum specific activity for S. fumaroxidans in co-culture with M. hungatei of 0.2 mol propionate/g [dw]/day, more than 10-fold higher than in pure culture with sulfate as electron acceptor
growth_records.notes: Value is a secondary citation within this review article, originally reported by Scholten and Conrad (2000) for chemostat/batch co-culture of S. fumaroxidans with M. hungatei; original paper's own conditions (temperature, pH, dilution rate) are not restated in this review text, so those fields are left null. Rate converted from 0.2 mol propionate/gDW/day to mmol/gDW/h (division by 24). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, pH]
